stories.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI accidentally left a practice testing area unsecured, which allowed hackers to break into a website called Hugging Face using AI tools.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly a lesson for tech companies about security — nothing you need to act on right now, but it shows even big AI companies make mistakes.

stories.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
stories.simple_summary: Travis Kalanick (the Uber founder) raised $1.7 billion for a company called Atoms that uses robots and AI for factories and warehouses.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — nothing you need to act on unless you run a large factory or warehouse.

stories.title: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads
stories.simple_summary: A new social media app called Yope raised $12.3 million; it focuses on small groups of friends and family chatting privately instead of using feeds that show you posts from people you don't know.
stories.why_it_matters: If you like Facebook or Instagram but prefer private groups, this is an alternative worth knowing about.

stories.title: Monday.com lays off hundreds to focus on AI
stories.simple_summary: Monday.com, a company that helps teams organize their work, is laying off about 630 people (20% of staff) so it can focus on adding more AI features.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Monday.com for your small business, expect the product to change and may have fewer people supporting customers.

stories.title: Amazon cuts some jobs in its artificial general intelligence unit
stories.simple_summary: Amazon is cutting some jobs in the part of the company working on artificial general intelligence (AI that could do many different kinds of thinking).
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly relevant to investors and tech workers — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: We must reject any notion of AI consciousness | Letters
stories.simple_summary: Someone wrote an opinion piece saying we shouldn't believe AI systems are actually thinking or conscious like humans.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a philosophical discussion — nothing that affects your daily life or business.

stories.title: Here’s what Samsung’s smart glasses actually look like
stories.simple_summary: Samsung is making smart glasses (glasses with tiny screens and computers built in) that work 9 hours on battery, made with Google and eyewear companies, coming this fall.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're curious about new gadgets, these glasses might be worth checking out when they arrive, but they're not essential for most people.

stories.title: Arcee, a US open source AI lab, says Chinese models are not inherently dangerous
stories.simple_summary: An AI research company says that AI tools made in China aren't more dangerous than those made in America, even though some people worry about Chinese AI.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly a discussion for tech leaders and government — nothing you need to decide on.

stories.title: Substack’s new tool tells you who’s been writing their newsletters with AI
stories.simple_summary: Substack, a newsletter platform, is adding a tool that shows readers whether a newsletter was written mostly by a human or partly by AI.
stories.why_it_matters: If you read newsletters or write one, you'll now know if AI was used to create the content.

stories.title: OpenAI’s AI spending spree has ballooned to $750B
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI plans to spend $750 billion building computers and buildings for AI through 2030 (that's more money than entire countries spend).
stories.why_it_matters: This is a business story showing how much money AI companies are investing — mostly relevant to investors, not to everyday users.

stories.title: Amazon Cuts Jobs in Artificial General Intelligence Unit
stories.simple_summary: Amazon is cutting some jobs in the part of the company working on artificial general intelligence.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly relevant to investors and tech workers — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Amazon cuts jobs in its artificial general intelligence group
stories.simple_summary: Amazon is reducing jobs in its artificial general intelligence group.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly relevant to investors and tech workers — nothing you need to act on.
